hero.title: LA Metro Map & Guide: Simple, Practical & Tourist‑Friendly
hero.subtitle: Your quick guide to navigating LA's Metro Rail without confusion — what it does, what it doesn't, and when to use it.

map.caption: Official LA Metro Rail Map — Updated 2026

howToRead: Colors represent Metro Rail lines, not neighborhoods.
Letters (A, B, D, E) indicate specific rail lines and services.
Solid dots show stations; interchange symbols show transfer points between lines.
7th Street/Metro Center and Union Station are the main downtown transfer hubs.
Single fare is $1.75 — always tap in and out with a TAP card.
Trains run roughly 4:30am–12:30am daily; check the Transit app for live arrivals.

routes.title: A Line (Blue) — DTLA to Long Beach
bullets: Runs from 7th Street/Metro Center in DTLA south to Long Beach.
Useful for visiting Long Beach (Aquarium of the Pacific, Pike Outlets).
Takes about 60 min end to end.
Runs 4:30am–12:30am daily.

routes.title: B Line (Red) — Hollywood & Universal Studios
bullets: The most useful line for tourists.
Runs from Union Station through DTLA up to Hollywood/Highland (Walk of Fame, TCL Theatre), Hollywood/Vine, and Universal City.
Single fare $1.75.
Takes 25 min from DTLA to Hollywood.

routes.title: D Line (Purple) — DTLA & Koreatown
bullets: Runs parallel to the B Line underground from Union Station through DTLA and west to Koreatown.
Extension to Westwood/UCLA opened 2025.
Single fare $1.75.
Useful for reaching Koreatown and the Wilshire corridor.

routes.title: E Line (Expo) — DTLA to Santa Monica
bullets: The single most tourist‑useful Metro line.
Runs from 7th Street/Metro Center in DTLA to Santa Monica (3 blocks from the pier).
Takes about 45 min and runs frequently.
$1.75 each way — far cheaper than parking.

routes.title: Fares & TAP Card
bullets: Single fare: $1.75. Day pass: $7 (unlimited rides).
TAP card (reloadable): $2 one‑time fee, available at any Metro station vending machine.
Download the TAP app to use your phone instead of a card.
Always tap in and out — inspectors issue $75–$250 fines.

safety: Use Metro for DTLA to Santa Monica (E Line) and DTLA to Hollywood (B Line) — it beats parking costs.
Use Uber for anything off these corridors, or if you're carrying bags.
Trains get emptier after 9pm — prefer Uber for late nights.
Use Google Maps transit mode for real‑time alerts and the right line.
Stand near other riders and well‑lit station areas after dark.
Avoid empty cars, even if they look convenient.
Metro cannot replace a car for most of LA's tourist sights — plan accordingly.

do: Let riders exit before you board.
Move into the car and away from the doors.
Have your TAP card or TAP app ready before the turnstile.
dont: Don't eat messy or strong‑smelling food.

apps.name: TAP App
apps.benefit: Official LA Metro app for loading fares and tapping your phone at turnstiles. Download before arriving.

apps.name: Google Maps
apps.benefit: Integrates Metro routes — use transit mode to find the right line and departure time.

apps.name: Transit App
apps.benefit: Real‑time arrivals for all LA Metro lines. More reliable than Metro's own arrivals board.

accessibility.title: Stations with Elevators
accessibility.content: Major hubs like Union Station and 7th Street/Metro Center offer full accessibility.

accessibility.title: Wheelchair‑Friendly Routes
accessibility.content: Most LA Metro Rail stations are step‑free; plan elevator‑only transfers at interchanges.

accessibility.title: Tips for Mobility Needs
accessibility.content: Allow extra time at downtown transfer hubs and use the Transit app to confirm elevator status.

mistakes: Assuming Metro reaches every LA tourist sight — it doesn't.
Forgetting to tap out and risking a fine.
Taking the train late at night when cars get empty.
Driving and paying for parking at Universal Studios instead of riding the B Line.

faq.q: Is the LA Metro useful for tourists?
faq.a: Yes for specific corridors: DTLA to Santa Monica (E Line) and DTLA to Hollywood/Universal (B Line). For everything else, a car or Uber is usually faster.

faq.q: How much does a Metro ride cost?
faq.a: A single ride is $1.75 with a TAP card, and a day pass is $7 for unlimited rides.

faq.q: Do I need a TAP card?
faq.a: Yes — a reloadable TAP card costs a $2 one‑time fee, or you can use the TAP app on your phone.

faq.q: Is the LA Metro safe at night?
faq.a: Trains get emptier after 9pm. Stay near other riders and well‑lit areas, or take an Uber for late nights.
